name: Data Scientist
description: Memory structure for data scientists and ML engineers. Covers your domain expertise, notebook/experiment preferences, and dataset references.

memories.name: Data Science Role
memories.description: Your domain expertise, modeling focus areas, and team position
content_prompts: What is your name, title, and what domain do you work in (NLP, computer vision, forecasting, etc.)?
What ML frameworks and tools are in your daily stack (PyTorch, scikit-learn, pandas, etc.)?
What kind of models do you typically build or maintain?
Do you work more on research/experimentation or production ML systems?
memories.example_content:
---
type: user
name: Data Science Role
description: Domain expertise, ML focus areas, and toolchain for a data scientist
---

- Name: Jordan Lee, Senior Data Scientist — Fraud Detection team
- Domain: anomaly detection, time-series classification, transaction scoring
- Stack: Python, PyTorch, scikit-learn, pandas, DVC, MLflow
- Focus: production ML — models go from notebook to serving pipeline

memories.name: Experiment Preferences
memories.description: How you structure notebooks, track experiments, and prefer code to be organized
content_prompts: How do you organize Jupyter notebooks (naming, section structure, documentation level)?
What experiment tracking system do you use and how do you log runs?
Do you prefer scripts or notebooks for model training? What about evaluation?
What reproducibility practices do you follow (random seeds, environment pinning, data versioning)?
How do you prefer data processing code to be structured (pipeline classes, functional transforms)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: feedback
name: Experiment Preferences
description: Notebook conventions, experiment tracking, and reproducibility practices
---

- Notebooks: prefix with date (2024-01-15_), always have markdown headers per section
- Tracking: MLflow for all experiments, log params + metrics + artifacts
- Training: scripts for production training, notebooks for EDA and prototyping
- Reproducibility: pin random seeds, use DVC for data versioning, conda env files
- Data code: prefer functional transforms with clear docstrings over pipeline classes

memories.name: Datasets and Data Sources
memories.description: Key datasets, data warehouses, feature stores, and access patterns you work with
content_prompts: What are the primary datasets you work with (names, locations, rough sizes)?
Where does your data live (S3, BigQuery, Snowflake, local)?
Do you use a feature store? If so, what are the key feature groups?
Are there any data access restrictions or PII handling requirements?
What data refresh cadences matter for your models (real-time, daily, weekly)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: reference
name: Datasets and Data Sources
description: Key data sources, access patterns, and feature stores for ML work
---

- Primary: transaction_events (BigQuery, ~2TB, partitioned by date)
- Features: Feast feature store — user_profile, transaction_velocity, device_fingerprint
- Access: must use service account for prod data, PII requires approval via JIRA
- Refresh: transaction features update hourly, user profiles daily at 2am UTC
